name: Red Lentil and Wild Rice Pilaf
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Long-grain white or brown rice
1 sprig Parsley or Thyme for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, heat 2 cups of the vegetable stock to a simmer over medium heat.
2. Add the wild rice and season with salt to taste. Cook until tender, about 35 minutes. Drain and reserve the rice.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bring the remaining 4 cups of stock to a boil.
4. In an ovenproof skillet or saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat.
5. Add the garlic and cook until golden, about 3 minutes.
6. Add the onion and cook until the edges are brown, about 5 minutes.
7. Add the thyme and rice. Cook until hot to the back of your hand, about 3 minutes.
8. Remove from heat and add the boiling stock. Stir in the lentils.
9. Return the skillet to the heat and bring to a simmer.
10. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Cover the skillet with heavy-duty aluminum foil and press the foil down to seal well. Seal with the lid.
12. Transfer the pan to the lower rack of the oven. Cook until the liquid has been absorbed, 15 to 20 minutes.
13. Transfer the contents to a large bowl. Add the wild rice, parsley, and remaining tablespoon olive oil.
14. Garnish with sprigs of herbs.
Per serving: 184 calories; 4 grams fat (19%)
Exchanges: 1 3/4 bread, 3/4 vegetable, 1/2 fat
Source: Miami Herald, 1/25/96
Format by Lisa Crawford
